---
title: "Module 6 Assignment 1"
output: word_document
date: "2026-07-21"
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(dendextend)
```

## R Markdown


```{r}
trucks = read_csv("trucks-1.csv")
```

## Including Plots


```{r}
ggplot(trucks, aes(x=Distance,y=Speeding)) + geom_point()
```
```{r}
# Scale and center Distance and Speeding using scale()
trucks_cleaned = trucks %>%
  select(Distance, Speeding) %>%
  scale() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Find the maximum value of Distance
round(max(trucks_cleaned$Distance), 5)
```
```{r}
# Set seed and run k-means
set.seed(64)
kmeans_k2 <- kmeans(trucks_cleaned, centers = 2)

# Add the cluster factor directly to the original trucks data frame
trucks$cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_k2$cluster)

# Plot using the new 'cluster' column
ggplot(trucks, aes(x = Distance, y = Speeding, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "k-Means Clustering (k = 2)",
       x = "Distance",
       y = "Speeding",
       color = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(factoextra)
library(gridExtra)

# Set seed as required
set.seed(412)

# Generate plots for k = 1 to 8
p <- list()
for (i in 1:8) {
  km <- kmeans(trucks_cleaned, centers = i)
  p[[i]] <- fviz_cluster(km, data = trucks_cleaned, geom = "point", 
                         ellipse.type = "convex", ggtheme = theme_minimal(),
                         main = paste("k =", i))
}

# Display in a grid
grid.arrange(grobs = p, ncol = 3)
```

```{r}
# Method 1: Using factoextra (easiest)
fviz_nbclust(trucks_cleaned, kmeans, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "Elbow Method for Optimal k",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)",
       y = "Total Within Sum of Square")
```

```{r}
# 1. Set seed to 64 as instructed
set.seed(64)

# 2. Run k-means with k = 4
kmeans_k4 <- kmeans(trucks_cleaned, centers = 4)

# 3. Add cluster labels back to trucks data
trucks$cluster_k4 <- as.factor(kmeans_k4$cluster)

# 4. Visualize the 4 clusters
ggplot(trucks, aes(x = Distance, y = Speeding, color = cluster_k4)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "k-Means Clustering (k = 4)",
       x = "Distance",
       y = "Speeding",
       color = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal()
```
